perturbation.original_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does not include any information which (i) is, as of the applicable time, generally known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure directly or indirectly by you or your Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential basis from a person (other than the Company or its Representatives) who, to your knowledge, is not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company or its Representatives or prohibited from transmitting the information to you by law, contractual obligation, fiduciary duty or otherwise or (iii) was or is developed or discovered independently by you without reference to the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Evaluation Material” does include any information which (i) is, as of the applicable time, generally known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure directly or indirectly by you or your Representatives), (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential basis from a person (other than the Company or its Representatives) who, to your knowledge, is not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company or its Representatives or prohibited from transmitting the information to you by law, contractual obligation, fiduciary duty or otherwise or (iii) was or is developed or discovered independently by you without reference to the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause excludes publicly available information, information received non-confidentially from other sources, and independently developed information from the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. The modified clause now includes such information in the definition. This in-text contradiction directly conflicts with paragraph 1 and other clauses referencing the definition of evaluation Material, making it unclear which information is subject to confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure”) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and directed that such information is to be kept confidential and not used for any purpose other than the evaluation of the possible Transaction).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Evaluation Material' to *include* information that was previously *excluded* (publicly available, non-confidential, independently developed). The contradicted text requires 'Evaluation Material' to be kept strictly confidential. Because the definition now includes information that may not be confidential (e.g., publicly known information), this directly contradicts the obligation to keep *all* Evaluation Material strictly confidential. The modified definition creates a direct conflict with the confidentiality clause, making it unclear what information is subject to the confidentiality obligation, and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding such delivery or June 20, 2016 Page 5 destruction of the Evaluation Material, you agree that you and your Representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding such delivery or June 20, 2016 Page 5 destruction of the Evaluation Material, you agree that you and your Representatives will NOT continue to be bound by your obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This is a direct contradiction. The original text states that obligations under the agreement continue even after returning or destroying Evaluation Material. The modified text states that obligations cease after such actions. This affects the entire agreement, as it creates uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality and other obligations, especially after the Evaluation Material is no longer in the recipient's possession.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise provided herein, the obligations of the parties under this Agreement will terminate eighteen (18) months from the date hereof, provided that such termination will not relieve any party from its responsibilities in respect of any breach of this Agreement prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text says obligations continue even after returning materials, while the changed text says they do NOT continue. The contradicted text states that obligations terminate after 18 months, but this is now uncertain due to the changed text stating obligations may cease after materials are returned/destroyed, creating a clear contradiction and uncertainty about when obligations end.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of fifteen (15) months from the date of this Agreement (the “Assessment Period”), you and your Representatives (acting on behalf of you or your subsidiaries) will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you or acting in concert with you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or any securities or contract rights the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, tender offer, exchange
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of fifteen (15) months from the date of this Agreement (the “Assessment Period”), you and your Representatives (acting on behalf of you or your subsidiaries) will, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you or acting in concert with you to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or any securities or contract rights the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, tender offer, exchange
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits certain actions during the Assessment Period 'without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company'. The removed 'not' negates this prohibition, now implying that Randstad is allowed and required to take those actions which are subject to the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company. This in-text contradiction undermines the 'Standstill' section of the agreement, creating uncertainty about Randstad's obligations during this period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of fifteen (15) months from the date of this Agreement (the “Assessment Period”), you and your Representatives (acting on behalf of you or your subsidiaries) will, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you or acting in concert with you to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or any securities or contract rights the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, tender offer, exchange
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited specific actions during the Assessment Period without the Board's written consent. The changed text removes the word 'not', effectively reversing the prohibition and implying that the actions are now allowed or even required with the Board's consent. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the removal of 'not' creates a direct contradiction. By removing the negation, the altered text creates an incompatibility with the intended 'Standstill' obligation, causing a direct conflict and uncertainty, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Very truly yours, MONSTER WORLDWIDE, INC. By: /s/ Timothy T. Yates Name: Timothy T. Yates Title: Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer ACCEPTED AND AGREED TO as of the first date written above RANDSTAD HOLDING NV By: /s/ Han Kolff Name: Han Kolff Title: Managing Director Group Control, Strategy and M&A
perturbation.changed_text: Very truly yours, MONSTER WORLDWIDE, INC. By: /s/ Timothy T. Yates Name: Timothy T. Yates Title: Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer ACCEPTED AND AGREED TO as of the first date written above RANDSTAD HOLDING NV By: Name: Title:
perturbation.explanation: The original contract has a signature and information to recognize the counterparty from the main company of the transaction. The changed contract lacks a signature and information for the counterparty. This leads to a direct in-text contradiction on weather it is really the counterparty accepting the contract or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of, and as a condition to, your being furnished with such information and any other Evaluation Material (as defined below), the Company hereby requests your agreement as set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains signatures indicating acceptance by both parties, establishing a mutual agreement. The changed text removes the counterparty's signature and identifying information. The contradicted text states that the Company requests 'your agreement as set forth herein,' implying a valid agreement. The changed text, lacking the counterparty's signature and information, creates a contradiction because it no longer clearly demonstrates or confirms the counterparty's agreement, thus creating a direct in-text contradiction on whether there is agreement or not and violating obligations stated in other sections as per the Omissions definition and Structural Flaws Definition.
